data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Zillow Group (ZG) After A 31.6% Year To Date Decline?
data.description:
• Wondering whether Zillow Group at around US$44.89 is pricing in too much hope or not enough, this article focuses squarely on what the current share price implies about value.
  • The stock has moved 10.0% over the past 7 days and 2.0% over the past 30 days. Yet year to date the return sits at a 31.6% decline, with a 35.2% decline over 1 year and a 65.8% decline over 5 years.
  • Investors have been reacting to ongoing headlines around the US housing market, changing mortgage affordability and the role of online platforms in how buyers, sellers and agents connect. These themes have kept attention on how Zillow Group’s business model lines up with broader real estate trends and housing activity.
  • Simply Wall St’s valuation checks give Zillow Group a 2 out of 6 valuation score. Next is a walkthrough of traditional valuation methods, followed by a final section that offers a more complete way to think about what the shares might be worth.

Approach 1: Zillow Group Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s dollars. It focuses on the cash the company might generate for shareholders rather than just current earnings or sales multiples.

For Zillow Group, the model starts with last twelve month free cash flow of about $137.7 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for several years, and Simply Wall St extends those projections further, using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. On this basis, Zillow’s free cash flow is projected at about $1.24b by 2030, with intermediate yearly estimates between 2026 and 2035 discounted back to present value.

Adding those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of $97.81 per share. Against the current share price of about $44.89, the model implies a 54.1% discount, which indicates that the shares are trading well below this cash flow based estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Zillow Group Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings can be uneven, the P/S ratio is often a cleaner way to think about value because it looks at what you are paying for each dollar of revenue, rather than profits that can swing around with accounting items or investment cycles.

Growth expectations and risk still matter, since investors usually accept a higher P/S multiple for businesses where they expect stronger revenue expansion and are comfortable with the risks, while slower or less certain growth tends to justify a lower multiple.

Zillow Group currently trades on a P/S of 4.17x. That is above both the Real Estate industry average of 2.48x and the peer average of 2.08x, which on the surface can make the shares look expensive compared with others in the space.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio of 3.53x is a proprietary estimate of what Zillow Group’s P/S might be given factors such as its growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk characteristics. data.title: Omnicom Interpublic Deal Reshapes Agencies With Synergies And Job Cuts
data.description:
• Omnicom Group (NYSE:OMC) has completed its $13b acquisition of Interpublic Group.
  • The company plans a major restructuring that includes 4,000 job cuts.
  • Omnicom will consolidate several agency brands as part of its post deal integration.

For investors tracking NYSE:OMC, this deal creates one of the largest combined marketing services groups, with a share price of $75.84 as of the last close. The stock is up 11.6% over the past 30 days, while the 5 year return stands at 18.5%.

The integration of Interpublic and the scale of job and brand cuts set up a period of significant change for clients and shareholders. As the restructuring unfolds, attention is likely to focus on how Omnicom manages client retention, cost savings and its broader agency portfolio under the enlarged group.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Omnicom Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Omnicom Group.

We've flagged 2 risks for Omnicom Group. See which could impact your investment.

This acquisition turns Omnicom into a much larger, more diversified marketing-services group at a time when clients are pushing hard on data driven, AI powered campaigns. By combining Interpublic’s agencies with Omnicom’s existing network, the group is aiming for US$1.50b in annual cost synergies, with US$900m targeted for 2026. For you as an investor, the question is whether those savings and any cross selling benefits offset the execution risk of merging two global holding companies while the broader market is dealing with persistent inflation and rate uncertainty.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Omnicom Group Narrative

  • The deal directly speaks to the narrative that a larger, data rich group can support growth in digital, analytics and AI powered services for global brands.
  • At the same time, integrating Interpublic introduces cultural and client retention risks that could challenge expectations for smooth margin improvement.
  • The scale of job cuts and brand consolidation may not be fully captured in earlier narratives that focused on the transaction before the integration plan was detailed.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Omnicom Group to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Large scale integration across thousands of employees and multiple agencies raises the risk of disruption, cost overruns and slower delivery of the planned US$1.50b in synergies.
  • ⚠️ Clients could reassess agency relationships as brands like Procter & Gamble or Coca Cola compare Omnicom’s combined offer with peers such as WPP and Publicis, particularly if service quality slips during restructuring.
  • 🎁 If the enlarged group achieves the targeted cost savings, profitability could benefit from a leaner structure and less duplication across agencies and back office functions.
  • 🎁 A broader portfolio and deeper data assets may help Omnicom compete for global accounts that seek integrated, omnichannel campaigns at scale.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on integration updates, especially around realized cost savings versus the US$1.50b target, and any commentary on client wins or losses as agencies are merged or rebranded. Market reaction to upcoming earnings, including any guidance on restructuring charges and synergy timing, will also be important in judging how quickly the combined group is settling. In parallel, wider market factors like inflation data and central bank policy can continue to influence sentiment toward advertising holding companies such as Omnicom, WPP and Publicis.

data.title: Knight Therapeutics Q4 Profit Tests Bullish Earnings Narrative After Yearlong Loss
data.description:
Advertisement

Knight Therapeutics FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Knight Therapeutics (TSX:GUD) has wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of C$133.1 million and basic EPS of C$0.09, capping a year where the trailing twelve month figures show C$450.1 million in revenue and a basic EPS loss of C$0.05. Over recent quarters, revenue has moved from C$96.9 million in Q4 2024 to C$88.1 million in Q1 2025, C$107.4 million in Q2 2025, C$121.5 million in Q3 2025 and C$133.1 million in Q4 2025. Quarterly EPS has ranged between a loss of C$0.13 and a profit of C$0.11, leaving investors focused squarely on how margins are holding up across the year.

See our full analysis for Knight Therapeutics.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed narratives around Knight Therapeutics's growth potential, profitability path and perceived risks.

See what the community is saying about Knight Therapeutics

Revenue climbs to C$450.1 million over the year

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Knight generated C$450.1 million of revenue, up from C$371.3 million at the end of FY 2024, while trailing twelve month net income moved from a profit of C$4.3 million to a loss of C$5.4 million.
  • Consensus narrative highlights strong organic revenue growth and a larger specialty portfolio as key positives, yet the shift from a trailing twelve month profit of C$4.3 million in Q4 2024 to a loss of C$5.4 million in Q4 2025 shows that higher sales have not yet translated into consistently positive earnings.

LTM EPS loss contrasts with bullish earnings forecasts

  • Even after a C$8.9 million net profit and C$0.09 EPS in Q4 2025, trailing twelve month EPS sits at a loss of C$0.05, compared with a trailing twelve month profit of C$0.04 per share a year earlier.
  • Bulls expect earnings to grow about 35.08% per year with profitability within three years, yet the current trailing twelve month loss of C$5.4 million and the data point that losses have grown at roughly 40.8% per year over five years mean the latest quarter has to be seen alongside a longer stretch of negative net margins.
    • This quarter’s C$8.9 million profit supports the bullish view that earnings can improve, but the trailing twelve month loss shows that one strong quarter has not yet reset the overall trend.
    • The bullish case leans on an analyst price target of C$8.03 versus today’s C$6.30 share price, so understanding how quickly trailing twelve month EPS moves from a C$0.05 loss toward positive territory is central to judging that view.

Some investors point to the strong Q4 profit and expected earnings growth as reasons to focus on the bullish case, while others are watching the trailing twelve month loss and five year loss trend just as closely. 🐂 Knight Therapeutics Bull Case

Valuation multiples balance optimism and past losses

  • The shares trade on a P/S of about 1.4x, compared with 0.9x for the Canadian pharmaceuticals industry and 1.2x for peers, while the DCF fair value in the dataset is C$85.16 and the analyst target used here is C$8.03 against a current price of C$6.30.
  • Bears argue that paying a higher P/S than peers is hard to justify while the company is still loss making, and the trailing twelve month loss of C$5.4 million alongside five year losses growing around 40.8% per year gives that concern real numbers behind it, even though models and analysts still see room between C$6.30 and C$8.03 for upside.
    • The higher P/S multiple fits with the idea that the market already prices in part of the expected 6.3% revenue growth and 35.08% earnings growth, which is what critics point to when they worry about downside if those forecasts are not met.
    • At the same time, the very large gap between the C$6.30 market price and the C$85.16 DCF fair value in the dataset shows why some investors see valuation support, even while bears focus on the recent period of negative net income.

Readers who are more cautious often focus on the higher P/S multiple and the recent loss history before deciding how comfortable they are with the valuation gap to C$8.03 and the much higher DCF fair value. 🐻 Knight Therapeutics Bear Case

GLOBALFOUNDRIES, trading at $42.47, is drawing attention in advanced semiconductor manufacturing as investors weigh its role in AI and data center infrastructure. That tighter loop can matter when optical interconnects are dealing with copper limits beyond 200G per lane, where reach, power use, and bandwidth density become critical for AI clusters. PepsiCo operates a large global snacks and beverages business, so water use and resource efficiency are central to its long term operations. Meeting its pep+ water goals ahead of the 2025 target adds a concrete ESG data point that investors can weigh alongside more traditional financial metrics. At the same time, the move into protein snacks with Good Warrior shows PepsiCo responding to consumer interest in higher protein, convenience focused options.

For shareholders in NasdaqGS:PEP, these updates sit alongside the usual questions around margins, pricing power, and capital allocation. data.title: Reassessing Allegion (ALLE) After A 19% Pullback And Mixed Valuation Signals
data.description:
• Wondering if Allegion at around US$144.66 is offering fair value or a margin of safety right now? This article walks through the key valuation signals that can help you decide.
  • The stock has seen a 1.4% decline over the last 7 days and a 19.4% decline over the last 30 days, even though the 1 year return sits at 14.0% and the 3 year return at 45.5%.
  • These moves come as Allegion continues to feature in broader discussions around building products and security solutions, which keeps it on the radar of both growth focused and income focused investors. With the share price pulling back recently while longer term returns remain positive, many readers are reassessing what a reasonable valuation range might look like.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Allegion currently holds a 5 out of 6 valuation score. The next sections break down the main valuation methods used, then finish with a way to think about valuation that goes beyond any single model.

Allegion delivered 14.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Building industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Allegion Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a single present value figure.

For Allegion, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flows in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow sits at about $686.7 million. Analyst and extrapolated projections in the model show annual free cash flow values between $693.2 million and $1,021.9 million over the next decade, with 2030 specifically projected at $856 million. Beyond the first five years, Simply Wall St extrapolates the figures rather than relying on additional analyst estimates.

After discounting all those projected cash flows back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $144.67 per share. With the current share price around $144.66, the implied discount is effectively 0.0%, which points to Allegion trading very close to this DCF estimate.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Allegion is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Allegion.

Approach 2: Allegion Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that business is currently generating. It helps you see how much the market is willing to pay for every dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how investors see growth and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty usually point to a lower one.

Allegion currently trades on a P/E of 19.36x. That sits just below the Building industry average of 20.21x and well below the peer group average of 41.04x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Allegion is 22.92x, which is its proprietary view of what a reasonable P/E might be after considering factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those company characteristics rather than relying on broad group averages. With Allegion’s actual P/E below the 22.92x Fair Ratio, the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, and Narratives bring this to life by letting you connect your view of Allegion’s story to a financial forecast, a fair value, and finally a comparison with the current share price. All of this is available within an easy tool on Simply Wall St’s Community page that millions of investors use to set their own assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins, see how those inputs translate into fair values that update automatically when fresh news or earnings arrive, and compare very different viewpoints. For example, a more optimistic Allegion Narrative might be built around recurring IoT and software driven security demand that supports a fair value closer to about US$180 per share. In contrast, a more cautious Allegion Narrative might lean on lower growth, more pressure in the Americas and less confidence in acquisitions, which might leave the user comfortable with a fair value nearer to the current price. This helps each investor decide for themselves whether Allegion looks more like a buy, a hold, or a potential trim based on their own story, not just a single model output.
